howItWorks.intro: This law of sines calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the Law of Sines?
faq.answer: The Law of Sines states that in any triangle, the ratio of a side to the sine of its opposite angle is constant: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B) = c/sin(C). It is used to solve triangles when you know an angle-side pair plus one additional measurement.

faq.question: When should I use the Law of Sines?
faq.answer: Use the Law of Sines when you know: (1) Two angles and one side (AAS or ASA), or (2) Two sides and a non-included angle (SSA, the ambiguous case). For SAS or SSS problems, use the Law of Cosines instead.

faq.question: What is the ambiguous case?
faq.answer: The ambiguous case (SSA) arises when you know two sides and a non-included angle. Depending on the values, there may be zero, one, or two valid triangles. Check if sin(B) = b·sin(A)/a yields a valid sine value.

faq.question: Can I solve a right triangle with the Law of Sines?
faq.answer: Yes, but for right triangles, SOH CAH TOA and the Pythagorean theorem are simpler. The Law of Sines is most useful for oblique (non-right) triangles.
